---
name: nezam-cache-strategies
description: HTTP, Redis, edge cache, and tag-based invalidation strategies that prevent stampedes and stale data.
version: 1.0.0
updated: 2026-05-08
changelog: []
---

# Purpose

Specify caching layers and invalidation contracts: HTTP, runtime cache (Redis/Vercel Runtime Cache), edge cache (Cloudflare/Vercel), and CMS-driven tag invalidation. Single-responsibility: cache policy + invalidation discipline.

# Inputs

- API contract from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-api-design/SKILL.md`.
- Gateway policy from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-api-gateway/SKILL.md`.
- CMS webhook spec from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-cms-integration/SKILL.md`.
- Performance budget from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-performance-optimization/SKILL.md`.

# Step-by-Step Workflow

1. Classify each route/data source: public-cacheable, private-cacheable, no-cache; pick TTL.
2. HTTP caching: set `Cache-Control: public, max-age=…, s-maxage=…, stale-while-revalidate=…`; use ETag/Last-Modified for revalidation.
3. Edge cache: tag responses with `Cache-Tag` (Vercel) or `Cache-Tag` (Cloudflare); plan purge endpoints.
4. Runtime cache: Redis or Vercel Runtime Cache; set jitter on TTLs to avoid stampedes; use single-flight (lock + fan-out).
5. Next.js 15: use `cacheTag`, `revalidateTag`, `unstable_cache` migration to `'use cache'` per skill rules.
6. Invalidation paths: webhook → tag purge; manual purge admin endpoint; deploy-time purge for global resets.
7. Document cache hierarchy and invalidation flow per route.

# Validation & Metrics

- Cache-hit ratio meets target per route (e.g., ≥ 90% for static content).
- p99 origin RPS stays within configured budget under traffic spike.
- Stale-while-revalidate keeps perceived TTFB < 50ms during refresh.
- Tag-purge propagates in ≤ 30s globally.
- No cache-key collisions across user-scoped data.

# Output Format

- `docs/core/required/sdd/CACHE_STRATEGY.md` (per-route TTLs, tags, invalidation).
- HTTP header presets per route.
- Redis key schema + lock pattern.
- Invalidation flow diagrams (mermaid).

# Integration Hooks

- `/PLAN api` produces caching map.
- `/SCAN perf` validates ratios.
- `/DEVELOP` consumes presets (without writing app code here).
- Pairs with `@.cursor/skills/nezam-api-design/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-api-gateway/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-cdn-optimization/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-vercel-deploy/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-cloudflare-edge/SKILL.md`.
- Honors `[.cursor/rules/workspace-orchestration.mdc](.cursor/rules/workspace-orchestration.mdc)`.

# Anti-Patterns

- Caching authenticated responses without `Vary` or scoped key.
- TTL = 0 disabling cache "to be safe" (cache stampede on every request).
- Single global purge for granular updates (over-invalidation).
- Using ETag without normalizing weak/strong comparisons.
- Hash-based cache keys that include nondeterministic fields.

# External Reference

- RFC 9111 HTTP Caching (https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9111) — current.
- Next.js 15 Cache Components & `revalidateTag` (https://nextjs.org/docs).
- Vercel Runtime Cache (https://vercel.com/docs).
- Cloudflare Cache & Cache-Tag (https://developers.cloudflare.com/cache/) — current.
- Closest skills.sh/official analog: cache-strategy / http-caching.
